title: Advanced and creative hair professional
change: Occupational standard and assessment plan revised
overviewOfRole: The practical application of advanced and creative hairdressing skills, personalised to client requirements, whilst providing an outstanding service.
entryRequirements: <p><span style="font-family: " open="" sans",="" arial,="" sans-serif;="" font-size:="" 15px;="" background-color:="" rgba(255,="" 255,="" 1)"="">English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the apprenticeship funding rules.&nbsp;</span></p>

knowledges.detail: The importance of complying with legal, industry and organisational requirements for safe, legal, and sustainable working practices and the impact that non-compliance can have on the salon business.
How to adapt services, products, methods, advice and guidance provided in response to complex analysis findings of the client's hair and scalp.
How salons support diverse clients and respect cultural differences.
The key considerations when conducting a detailed evaluation of technical services including a review of any adaptations and recommendation made for future services for clients.
Communication techniques to give and receive information, adapting style to audience, and overcoming barriers to communication.
How to monitor individuals' health and wellbeing pre, during and post service and when and how to implement the correct course of action.
How promotions, sale of products, further services and personalised recommendations can be used to target client's desired outcomes.
The different approaches to meeting targets in the hair salon including within agreed timescales, reviewing and recording progress to build on future targets.
The legal responsibilities around using images found and created while researching fashion forward trends.
The legal responsibilities of using social media platforms within a salon business environment.

The principles of target marketing and the factors that have to be considered when creating a targeted marketing plan, including location, environment, local amenities, existing client base, competitors, popular and profitable services.
Social media marketing strategies and plans and how they can be used effectively by a salon business.
The sector specific organisations that provide guidance and support to the hair sector.

How to investigate and map relevant publications, social media pages and channels, websites, networks, educational events and updates from trade organisations to stay informed and connected within the industry.
The use of reflective practice theories and techniques to inform professional development of an individual and improve approaches to practice.
The benefits of effective communication, relationship management and team collaboration.
The types of salon software systems available and their advantages and limitations.
The stages of creating a portfolio of advanced hair looks reflecting individual creativity.
The stages of planning, designing, creating, producing, and presenting an image considering factors such as design plan, purpose and message for photoshoot image or competition entry.
The principles and elements of creating a total look, including the role of hair, clothing and overall image.
The methods of presenting, showcasing, and promoting the fashion forward portfolio of advanced hair looks using different media whilst targeting a specific market, individuals or groups.
The principles and tools for evaluation and the importance of evaluations to improve the business.
How to adapt individual hairstyles taking into consideration the client's requirements.
How and when to use advanced sectioning and cutting techniques when conducting bespoke cutting services on hair above and below shoulders.
The impact of tension, degree of elevation and cutting method on different hair classifications.
The procedures and techniques for colour conversion services and how they can be adapted safely for bespoke services.

Specialist products, tools and tests used in colour conversion services including pre-pigmentation that is light to dark, pre-lightening; dark to light; removing bands of colour; correcting highlights.
The scientific principles of hair colour conversion services and techniques to include pre-pigmentation and lightening progression chart.
The effect on the hair of not completing pre and post treatments when carrying out advanced colour conversion services and the impact of this on the final result.
The specialist products and techniques used in hair colour conversion services and how and when to use them, including tools, materials, equipment, sectioning techniques, application techniques.
The scientific principles of colour products on the hair structure.
How to apply scientific principles to blend tone and shade the hair.
The legal requirements and manufacturer's instructions relating to creative and advanced colour conversion products.
How to remove artificial colour.
How to remove bands of colour.
How to recolour hair previously treated with lighteners using pre-pigmentation.
How to recolour hair previously treated with permanent colour that has had artificial colour removed.
How to correct highlights and lowlights whilst retaining a highlight or lowlight effect.
The methods of applying and removing colour products.
The personalised sectioning techniques to correct the previous colour result and achieved the desired look.
Approaches to overcoming bespoke colouring complications, taking into account client skin tone and hair texture.
Approaches to overcoming colour correction complications, taking into account client skin tone and hair texture.
The tertiary colours that effectively neutralise unwanted colour.
The pre and post treatments that enhance a colour correction service.
The importance of restoring the hair's pH balance after the colour correction service.
The types and causes of colour correction problems that may occur during processing and how to rectify them.
The safety considerations and results of hair tests which must be taken into account when smoothing and strengthening hair.
The scientific principles of smoothing and strengthening systems.
The effects of smoothing and strengthening products on the hair structure.
The types of smoothing and strengthening systems, products, tools, equipment.
The application and removal of smoothing and strengthening products.
The effects of thermal styling tools during the service.
The precautions, considerations and benefits of smoothing and strengthening services.
The importance of responding to influencing factors that affect a smoothing and strengthening services.
The process of smoothing and strengthening hair including the effects of straightening iron temperature on the hair and the number of passes to create different results.
The importance of applying the correct temperature to the hair and the impact texture has on selecting the temperature of straightening irons.
The actions to take in the event of an adverse reaction to tests and contra indications.
The aftercare advice and recommendations for future services.
How and where to obtain historical, cultural and fashion inspiration to develop a given brief, to create visualisation for the client, including story board, mood board, concept board.
How hair preparation for different hairstyles is influenced by the hair type and texture.
How different factors affect the styling and dressing process and how choices can be visualised using a concept board, mood board, story board or brief.
How to prepare and select products, thermal styling tools and equipment.
The importance of following protocols when securing additional consent from the client for the proposed services and products.
When, why and how to apply different types of securing equipment, accessories and added hair.
The impact of excessive tension on the scalp and hair.
The principles of blending added hair seamlessly into the natural hair to achieve a flawless and cohesive appearance.
The chemical ingredients and their effects on the hair of styling products.

behaviours.detail: Effective communicator: chooses the most appropriate way of communicating with clients; helpful and courteous at all times; adapts behaviour in response to each client and situation, uses tact, diplomacy and compromise when required.
Considerate, empathetic and able to be objective in order to meet a client's individual needs.
Uses a strong understanding of additional products and services to effectively negotiate a beneficial after care regime for the client.
Provides outstanding customer service that results in repeat custom and client retention.
Demonstrates and encourages curiosity to foster new ways of thinking and working.

skills.detail: Provide advice on technical salon services that complies with legal, industry and organisational requirements.
Comply with legal, industry and organisational requirements incorporating safe, and sustainable working practices and workplace values in the provision of salon services.
Adapt style of communication to overcome barriers.

Conduct client consultations using hair and scalp assessments and analysis to provide a service that compliments the needs of the individual including detailed hair care service plan, product and treatment recommendations, suggested adaptations to requested services where required.
Agree adaptations to client services and ensure they align with equality, diversity, and cultural requirements.
Confirm the planned look has been achieved.
Conduct detailed evaluations of technical services with recommendations of future services for clients.

Promote, and link sell complementary products and services following delivery of prescriptive service plan providing clear guidance on how customers can extend and maximise the benefits of the service.
Adjust performance to meet sales and additional service targets consistently within the agreed timescale by reviewing recorded progress.
Use social media to promote salon services.
Use a range of resources to stay informed of current, emerging and evolving industry trends, terminology and equipment.
Engage in professional development activities to improve own professional practice.
Use social media and other platforms in the planning and design of a fashion forward portfolio of advanced hair looks.
Create an image for your fashion forward portfolio, via a photoshoot or competition.
Work collaboratively, contributing ideas and challenges where appropriate.
Present the vision and creativity behind the fashion forward portfolio of advanced hair looks to colleagues or stakeholders.
Present and promote a fashion forward portfolio of advanced hair looks using strategic choice of media to target specific markets, individuals or groups.
Utilise appropriate evaluation tools to assess the success and effectiveness of the final hair portfolio, providing valuable insights for future improvements and developments.
Produce precision and personalised looks using cutting techniques that achieve the desired results.
Perform cross-checks of haircuts to ensure an accurate and balanced result is achieved.
Plan creative colour conversion services to change the depth and tone of the client's hair.
Perform creative advanced colour conversion services, using bespoke colour formulations to change the depth and tone of the hair.
Resolve colour complications that occur during a colour service, by applying bespoke colour formulations techniques.
Use sectioning techniques to personalise the colour result and enhance the finished look.
Carry out creative advanced colour correction services, using bespoke colour formulations.
Use hair assessments to determine the amount of smoothing required.
Apply smoothing and strengthening techniques.
Apply smoothing systems, distributing the product evenly in line with manufacturer instructions and guidance.
Apply correct blow dry techniques and tension to apply the required degree of smoothing.
Apply correct section size, degree of tension timing and heat settings of tools to achieve desired result.
Apply straightening irons set at the correct temperature for the hair texture or curl pattern and completes the appropriate number of passes to gain a desired look.
Adapt smoothing and strengthening systems to take account of relevant factors that may affect services and problems that may occur.
Provide clients with advice and recommendations on the services provided.
Create a professionally finished event hair up style, minimum of 80%, using a minimum of three advanced styling techniques that enhance the finished result to meet event requirements.
Create a precise advanced hairstyle using tools, products and own dexterity to produce a polished, eye-catching look on above the shoulder hair.
Select a temporary hair attachment and suitably apply, blending the client's own hair effectively whilst taking into consideration hair and scalp condition.
Integrate accessories securely in a way that emphasises the desired look.
Provide appropriate instructions for post service maintenance.
Adapt techniques which minimise the risk of damage to the hair and scalp.
Remove temporary hair attachments that were used to create the look safely, ensuring minimal disruption to the natural hair.

eQAProvider.contactAddress:
Ofqual
Earlsdon Park, 53-55 Butts Road, Coventry, CV1 3BH

englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Clean, condition, cut, style, finish, and colour hair including facial hair

duties.dutyDetail: Promote sales and additional services to enhance the customer experience and overall treatment results.
Undertake business and personal development within the hair sector.
Craft a portfolio of advanced hair looks in order to showcase own skills and promote salon business or self, via a media platform.
Carry out precision and personalised cutting tailored to individual client characteristics, to create and enhance a collection of looks.
Resolve colour complications through applying bespoke colouring services including bespoke colour formulations, colour conversion and colour correction.
Carry out smoothing and strengthening services.
Style and finish hair for events.
